name: Paul Hindemith
abstract: Paul Hindemith ( POWL HIN-də-mit; German: [ˌpaʊ̯l ˈhɪndəmɪt] ; 16 November 1895 – 28 December 1963) was a German and American composer, music theorist, teacher, violist, and conductor. He founded the Amar Quartet in 1921, touring extensively in Europe.

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWEE] Hindemith, yeah. [INTERVIEWER] Yeah and I haven't seen you mention him as an influence. I am just wondering if his music is something that influenced you? -- [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, I haven't tried that one yet
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Putter Smith
about_quotes.evidence: there was one profound book by Paul Hindemith called Traditional Harmony. So I attribute my knowledge of the interior of harmony and voice leading and all that stuff from that
about_quotes.doc_title: Putter Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 27, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: J.J. Johnson remembers me as that little kid that was around reading Hindemith books... I was reading this stuff early.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: I succeeded Paul Hindemith, who was one of the greatest composers of the twentieth century, as a teacher there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: I bought three of his theory books when I was starting out, and that’s how I learned theory was Hindemith.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]
